name: IGH_DataAccess
summary: Provides access to component parameters, in order to retrieve and assign data during solutions.

methods.name: AbortComponentSolution
methods.summary: Call this method if you wish to stop solving this component.

methods.name: BlitData<Q>
methods.signature: bool BlitData<Q>(int paramIndex, GH_Structure<Q> tree, bool overwrite) where Q : IGH_Goo
methods.summary: Creates a direct copy of an existing data structure. The existing generic instance must of the exact same type as the target parameter.

params.name: paramIndex
params.doc: Index of output parameter.

params.name: tree
params.doc: Parameter whose data to blit

params.name: overwrite
params.doc: If true, any existing data will be removed.

methods.name: DisableGapLogic()
methods.summary: Call this function if you want to disable the gap null logic for the current iteration.

methods.name: DisableGapLogic(Int32)
methods.summary: Call this function if you want to disable the gap null logic for a specific output parameter.

params.name: paramIndex
params.doc: Index of output parameter to disable.

methods.name: GetData<T>(Int32, T)
methods.signature: bool GetData<T>(int index, ref T destination)
methods.summary: Retrieve data from an input parameter during GH_Component.SolveInstance().

returns.doc: True on success, false on failure.

methods.name: GetData<T>(String, T)
methods.signature: bool GetData<T>(string name, ref T destination)
methods.summary: Retrieve data from an input parameter during GH_Component.SolveInstance().
methods.remarks: Parameter access by index is faster, consider using the other overload if possible.

returns.doc: True on success, false on failure.

methods.name: GetDataList<T>(Int32, List<T>)
methods.summary: Retrieve an entire list of data from an input parameter during GH_Component.SolveInstance().

returns.doc: True on success, false on failure.

methods.name: GetDataList<T>(String, List<T>)
methods.summary: Retrieve an entire list of data from an input parameter during GH_Component.SolveInstance().
methods.remarks: Parameter access by index is faster, consider using the other overload if possible.

returns.doc: True on success, false on failure.

methods.name: GetDataTree<T>(Int32, GH_Structure<T>)
methods.signature: bool GetDataTree<T>(int index, out GH_Structure<T> tree) where T : IGH_Goo
methods.summary: Retrieve an entire data tree from an input parameter during GH_Component.SolveInstance().

returns.doc: True on success, false on failure.

methods.name: GetDataTree<T>(String, GH_Structure<T>)
methods.signature: bool GetDataTree<T>(string name, out GH_Structure<T> tree) where T : IGH_Goo
methods.summary: Retrieve an entire data tree from an input parameter during GH_Component.SolveInstance().
methods.remarks: Parameter access by index is faster, consider using the other overload if possible.

returns.doc: True on success, false on failure.

methods.name: IncrementIteration
methods.summary: Increments the iteration count by 1.

methods.name: ParameterTargetIndex
methods.summary: Get the target index for the specified output parameter.

params.name: paramIndex
params.doc: Index of output parameter.

methods.name: ParameterTargetPath
methods.summary: Get the target path for the specified output parameter.

params.name: paramIndex
params.doc: Index of output parameter.

methods.name: SetData(Int32, Object)
methods.summary: Stores data in an output parameter during GH_Component.SolveInstance(). Use this function only for setting individual data items. If you want to set lists of data, you *must* call SetDataList() instead.

params.name: paramIndex
params.doc: Index of the output parameter at which to store the data.

params.name: data
params.doc: Data to store. Data will be converted if necessary, but it will not be duplicated automatically.

methods.name: SetData(String, Object)
methods.summary: Stores data in an output parameter during GH_Component.SolveInstance().
methods.remarks: Parameter access by index is faster, consider using the other overload if possible.

params.name: paramName
params.doc: Name of the output parameter at which to store the data.

params.name: data
params.doc: Data to store. Data will be converted if necessary, but it will not be duplicated automatically.

methods.name: SetData(Int32, Object, Int32)
methods.signature: bool SetData(int paramIndex, Object data, int itemIndexOverride)
methods.summary: Expert user function. Stores data in an output parameter during GH_Component.SolveInstance(). Use this function only for setting individual data items. If you want to set lists of data, you *must* call SetDataList() instead.

params.name: paramIndex
params.doc: Index of the output parameter at which to store the data.

params.name: data
params.doc: Data to store. Data will be converted if necessary, but it will not be duplicated automatically.

params.name: itemIndexOverride
params.doc: Typically the item index is a result of Data matching logic. If you want to override the item index, then call this function.

methods.name: SetDataList(Int32, IEnumerable)
methods.summary: Stores a list of data in an output parameter during GH_Component.SolveInstance().

params.name: paramIndex
params.doc: Index of the output parameter at which to store the list.

params.name: data
params.doc: Data to store. Data will be converted if necessary, but it will not be duplicated automatically.

methods.name: SetDataList(String, IEnumerable)
methods.summary: Stores data in an output parameter during GH_Component.SolveInstance().
methods.remarks: Parameter access by index is faster, consider using the other overload if possible.

params.name: paramName
params.doc: Name of the parameter at which to store the data.

params.name: data
params.doc: Data to store. Data will be converted if necessary, but it will not be duplicated automatically.

methods.name: SetDataList(Int32, IEnumerable, Int32)
methods.signature: bool SetDataList(int paramIndex, IEnumerable data, int listIndexOverride)
methods.summary: Expert user function. Stores a list of data in an output parameter during GH_Component.SolveInstance().

params.name: paramIndex
params.doc: Index of the output parameter at which to store the list.

params.name: data
params.doc: Data to store. Data will be converted if necessary, but it will not be duplicated automatically.

params.name: listIndexOverride
params.doc: Typically the branch index is a result of Data matching logic. If you want to override the branch index, then call this function.

methods.name: SetDataTree(Int32, IGH_DataTree)
methods.summary: Folds a utility tree representation into this tree.

params.name: paramIndex
params.doc: Index of output parameter to merge with.

params.name: tree
params.doc: Tree to merge

methods.name: SetDataTree(Int32, IGH_Structure)
methods.summary: Folds a utility tree representation into this tree.

params.name: paramIndex
params.doc: Index of output parameter to merge with.

params.name: tree
params.doc: Tree to merge

methods.name: Util_CountNonNullRefs<T>
methods.summary: Count all object references in L

params.name: L
params.doc: List to parse

returns.doc: Number of proper objects in L

methods.name: Util_CountNullRefs<T>
methods.summary: Count all null references in L

params.name: L
params.doc: List to parse

methods.name: Util_EnsureNonNullCount<T>
methods.summary: Tests a list to see if it contains sufficient non-null references.

params.name: L
params.doc: List to parse

returns.doc: True if L contains N or more non-null references.

methods.name: Util_FirstNonNullItem<T>
methods.summary: Returns the index of the first non-null item in a list.

params.name: L
params.doc: List to parse.

returns.doc: The index of the first non-null item or -1 if no object reference could be found.

methods.name: Util_RemoveNullRefs<T>
methods.summary: Remove all null references from a list of items.

params.name: L
params.doc: List to filter

returns.doc: List containing only non-null items. References are shared with L

properties.name: Iteration
properties.summary: Gets the current iteration count. The first time the SolveInstance() function is called on a component during a solution the Iteration counter will be zero. It will be incremented by one for every subsequent call.
